subject: mathematics

core_competencies.justification: The entire chapter introduces zero and negative quantities as numbers and develops arithmetic operations (addition and subtraction) on them, culminating in Brahmagupta's explicit rules for all integer operations in section 10.5 — matching this competency almost verbatim.

Section 10.1 explicitly constructs the full integer number line by extending the number ray leftward from 0, and students compare, order, and place integers on it across multiple Figure it Out sets, including the unmarked number line technique in section 10.1.

adjunct_competencies.justification: Section 10.5 (A Pinch of History) specifically names Brahmagupta's Brāhma-sphuṭa-siddhānta (628 CE) and quotes his rules for integer arithmetic, and also references Kautilya and the Bakśhālī manuscript as Indian contributions to the development of negative numbers.

Section 10.4 presents two open-ended puzzle structures — the hollow integer grid (completing grids to meet a border sum, finding multiple solutions) and the amazing grid (discovering that the circled-number sum is invariant regardless of choices) — where students discover their own solutions and investigate the underlying structure.

The amazing grid activity (section 10.4) requires inductive reasoning to notice the fixed sum across all choices, and section 10.5's Figure it Out asks students to explain Brahmagupta's subtraction rules using the number line, engaging deductive argument production.

dissolution_test: This chapter builds the student's ability to extend the number system to integers and compute with them by introducing negative numbers through concrete real-world models (a multi-storied building, a mine, bank accounts, temperature), constructing the full integer number line, and applying Brahmagupta's rules for addition and subtraction of positive numbers, negative numbers, and zero.
